THE CBO METHOD
This is my preferred scaling method for 2019.
For reference CBO = Campaign Budget Optimisation. Setting budgets at
campaign level.
1. Grad Test audiences and ads as per normal to find winning ad sets and
ads
2. Dupe winning adsets/ads to a SCALE Campaign (WC) using shared
Post IDs as per the Graduation Framework.
3. Test traction here using $50/d budgets minimum ($100/d + is better for
faster validation) - though you can get away with smaller budgets this can
impact the quality of data and time it takes, cutting bad ads/ad sets via Ads
Sniper (I'm mainly looking at early metrics $/ATC initially)
4. Once you've reached more than 5 sales profitably duplicate the winning
ad sets/ads and create the same audiences in a CBO - ensure you select a
1 day click window for optimisation and keep audiences grouped so
interests in one CBO, 1-3% lookalikes in another, 3-6% in another 7-10% in
another because of the audience quality - CBOs collect data at the
campaign level and this seems to provide better stability.
5. As a rule of thumb CBOs work better with larger budgets so start with
$50 minimum PER AD SET, ideally $100/d+. Optimise for lowest cost and
if possible, highest value (if running multiple priced products). I don't mind
setting budgets higher because I will set rules to pause out bad
performance.
6. CBOs optimise faster running only 2 ads per ad set. Have others ready
in waiting or make dupes of winning ads if needed. If I have higher budgets
I'll go with 3 ads.
7. If required you can run the same ad sets/ads in a PPE campaign to bulk
up social proof.
8. Use rules or manually cut out bad ads in the CBO using Ads Sniping
manually or through automations
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

9. Give the CBO 48 hours to find optimal performance - restart ads that
didn't perform, the next day, manually or using rules. If they fail to perform 3
days in a row don't restart the ads
10. Facebook have confirmed CBO budget increases don't impact the
learning phase so with this in mind you can increase budgets as you like.
However it DOES impact the pacing algorithm, so increasing from $100/d
to $1000/d will throw more money at the ad sets and it's hit and miss how
Facebook assigns this. I would recommend increasing budget when the
CBO has enough history and today's performance is good too. Facebook
will try spend it based on history but it can cause some fluctuations. You
need to decide whether you let it play out, or continue pausing bad ads,
restarting the next day. I wouldn't recommend changing budget too
frequently.
11. If you wish to use manual bid, duplicate the whole CBO and test
manual bid - it's an effective way to run multiple bids for each ad set to find
the best manual bid each day.
12. If you wish to break the CBO into segments for example when an ad
set is performing really well for a particular age range or placement, you
are effectively starting the ad set again. Facebook favour recent history so
even if your account and campaign is mature it will take time to relearn.
You can H-Scale into an existing CBO but it's not without it's risks of
upsetting performance on existing ad sets. Worth pushing to a new CBO
unless you have spend/history to absorb any disruption
13. If you're running a short-term promotion like Black Friday, Valentine's or
others and you want to use a strong CBO, then duplicate it on lower budget
and build it back up - the reason being that you don't want to have to keep
pausing/restarting ads for promotions. I use a 'Promotions' CBO specifically
for fixed time promotions.
14.CBO is not a short-cut to testing, but a powerful tool for scaling!
The video below demonstrates the strategy and the exact rules I use will be
published below.
